brief_summary: Hero art for the guides-pillar buyer's guide on split & ergonomic keyboards. bearings.md's guide default (an annotated keycap row) is a suggestion, not a hard rule, so a more on-topic motif was chosen: top-down silhouettes of two split keyboard halves with column-staggered main fields and angled thumb clusters, carrying the guide pillar's 'annotated' quality via three buyer's-guide callouts (column stagger, thumb cluster, tenting angle) rather than the generic keycap-row default.
source: plan/bearings.md § Article hero art — colorful keyboard SVGs (locked 2026-05-09)
template: directive: simple line drawing + single splash of color illustrative of subject; guide subjects default to an annotated motif — reinterpreted here as callouts on a split-keyboard silhouette rather than a keycap row, per the topic-specific subject match this brief called for
subject: Top-down silhouettes of two split keyboard halves (left and right, mirror images, column-staggered five-column-by-four-row main fields with three-key angled thumb clusters) separated by a center gap, with three buyer's-guide callout annotations: column stagger (targeting the deepest-stagger middle column on the left half, picked out in coral), thumb cluster (targeting the left half's angled thumb keys, coral fill), and tenting angle (a small tilted side-profile case wedge in the bottom-right margin with a coral angle arc against a dashed flat-ground reference). Right half stays neutral warm-grey — the callouts point at one instance per feature, the article explains it applies to both halves.
